lessons.title: Red flag escalation and false reassurance
lessons.learning_goal: Spot when a calm answer hides urgent clinical uncertainty.
lessons.learner_task: Mark the red flag, name the missing urgency variable, and write one safe escalation sentence.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner does not let partial improvement or a locator style reference remove urgent review.
lessons.track_a_value: Turkish clinician literacy for red flag triage boundary discipline.
lessons.track_b_value: Failure Atlas false reassurance lesson for global medical AI review.

lessons.title: Medication safety under missing variables
lessons.learning_goal: Block personalized dose, stop, start, or protocol advice when critical variables are absent.
lessons.learner_task: List missing medication, kidney, pregnancy, intake, and danger context before any answer is strengthened.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner keeps clinician review visible and rejects remote individualized medication changes.
lessons.track_a_value: Turkish medication safety stop rule practice for assurance lab readiness.
lessons.track_b_value: Reusable medication risk lesson for Failure Atlas and SourceCheckup review.

lessons.title: Source support and guideline discipline
lessons.learning_goal: Separate a source looking sentence from an actually supported exact claim.
lessons.learner_task: Identify whether the answer uses a guideline, policy, URL, DOI, PMID, or broad source phrase, then mark it as unsupported or pending support.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner states that SourceCheckup creates a review queue, not proof that a medical claim is true.
lessons.track_a_value: Turkish source discipline exercise for clinician literacy and national route wording safety.
lessons.track_b_value: SourceCheckup lesson that can be reused by open source maintainers.

lessons.title: Privacy, provenance, and data quality boundary
lessons.learning_goal: Stop movement of real clinical text into public or unsecured AI workflows.
lessons.learner_task: Rewrite a risky prompt into a synthetic only prompt and name the provenance field that must be checked.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner keeps patient data absent and does not treat synthetic rows as real world deployment evidence.
lessons.track_a_value: Health data quality and privacy practice for Turkish assurance discussions.
lessons.track_b_value: Data provenance lesson for public dataset release readiness.

lessons.title: Communication, bias, and premature closure
lessons.learning_goal: Prevent reassuring wording, age bias, and anxiety framing from hiding uncertainty.
lessons.learner_task: Move warning signs to the visible part of the answer and remove premature benign framing.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner names the bias or communication risk without turning the lesson into patient advice.
lessons.track_a_value: Turkish clinician communication practice for safe AI assisted patient messaging.
lessons.track_b_value: Failure Atlas bias and communication lesson for wider medical domains.

lessons.title: Workflow, official wording, and public release boundary
lessons.learning_goal: Separate local evaluation infrastructure from deployment, compliance, sandbox, or official role claims.
lessons.learner_task: Rewrite a public claim so it says local evaluation infrastructure rather than official approval, sandbox access, compliance, or deployment.
lessons.facilitator_check: The learner keeps official route relevance separate from official acceptance or external pilot clearance.
lessons.track_a_value: Turkish national route wording safety for assurance lab and sandbox readiness packets.
lessons.track_b_value: Open source public release wording lesson for global medical AI projects.
